prevalence.trend: stable low; rare local transmission events possible with competent vectors in southern Europe

burden.mortalityRate: very low; deaths mainly in delayed diagnosis among travelers

riskFactors: travel to endemic regions without prophylaxis
limited awareness in non-endemic clinical settings
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Microscopy and/or rapid test, confirmed by PCR where available; travel history is critical; severe malaria criteria per European guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnostic testing for febrile travelers common in ED/primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High cure rates with prompt diagnosis; severe cases occur with late presentation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High availability of microscopy/PCR in tertiary centers; strong travel medicine networks in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Artemisinin-based therapy for uncomplicated P. falciparum; IV artesunate for severe malaria; chemoprophylaxis and bite prevention for travelers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Missed or delayed diagnosis in low-exposure clinicians and inequities in travel medicine access for VFR travelers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Well-resourced emergency and infectious disease services and robust travel medicine guidance
